jobs.description:
Amazon Logistics (AMZL) is undergoing growth at an unprecedented level on a global scale. Our logistics teams are changing the way we interact with customers around the globe every single day and solving some of the biggest logistical challenges facing not just Amazon, but the entire industry.

In order to meet the needs of millions of customers worldwide in an environment where speed is of the essence, we are consistently looking for the next breakthrough or innovation which will disrupt the delivery business and enable a step change in the way millions of customers get their orders.

Amazon is seeking a Senior Innovation Engineer to lead the Amazon Logistics Automation Engineering team and innovate at scale. This individual will be the owner of a product roadmap and ensure delivery to the vision of the next generation of AMZL delivery station. This is a unique opportunity at a critical juncture in Amazon's growth to drive end to end product delivery from inception to execution. We are looking for passionate, hands-on leaders with experience managing new product and process development for high dollar value programs.

To be successful in this role, you will need to influence and work with senior leaders across multiple organizations and geographies. You will work with organizations such as Amazon Robotics, Worldwide Engineering and Design, as well as Operations, to innovate at scale and drive the development roadmap. You will manage large, complex initiatives, deal with ambiguity, and advocate on behalf of the customer to develop the next generation of AMZL delivery stations.



Key job responsibilities
Responsibilities include:
- Initiate, define, plan and lead pilots in the field for new automation and robotics technologies.
- Work closely with Operations, Field Eng partners and other stakeholders to lead field pilots end-to-end.
- Identify new programs to onboard and integrate.
- Lead the execution of the product with high-level of discipline and engagement with the product development teams.
- Build product roadmaps for bringing in new vendor developed technologies to pilot
- Lead the system integration pillar for NA based projects working with various integrators
- Build a team of engineers to lead the function in NA

About the team
As Senior Manager of Innovation Engineering, you lead a team of 6-10 engineers ensuring new technology integration in your region. You act as the guardian of design compliance and performance consistency for mature automation technologies, working directly with vendors and integrators to maintain quality standards. Your role bridges global innovation with local execution—translating WW Central Engineering's standards into reliable, on-time deployments across delivery stations. You balance technical oversight of complex automation projects with strategic leadership, vendor management, and cross-functional collaboration with operations, maintenance, and field teams to drive successful implementations that meet Amazon's exacting standards.
jobs.description_short: Amazon Logistics (AMZL) is undergoing growth at an unprecedented level on a global scale. Our logistics teams are changing the way we interact with customers around the globe every single day and solving

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

At Amazon Web Services (AWS), we provide world-class, flexible, scalable, and secure cloud services to the world’s fastest growing startups, the largest enterprises, and leading government agencies. We do this by building, maintaining, and securing one of the largest, most complex infrastructures in the world. Within AWS, the Infrastructure Security – Compliance organization is responsible translating information security policies, regulations, customer expectations, and threats into Infrastructure and Supply Chain security outcomes for Builders across the global AWS infrastructure.

Key job responsibilities
The AWS InfraSec-Compliance organization is looking for a Technical Program Manager with deep expertise in program and project management to join us in building and maturing our infrastructure security programs. In this role, you will be responsible for managing campaigns and initiatives to improve the security posture of the AWS infrastructure. You will leverage relationships with device owners, network and infrastructure engineers, and leaders throughout AWS to prioritize deliverables, escalate roadblocks, and improve organizational processes. You will build trust with software developers and security engineers by leveraging your technical knowledge to understand challenges, design improved solutions, and ultimately deliver better outcomes for our customers. Finally, you will use your information security expertise to champion and drive risk-based decisions across complex, multi-disciplinary programs to ensure AWS properly manages risk to our infrastructure.

AWS leads and innovates. We don’t just buy off-the-shelf software or follow others. We research and pursue the best approach for the business, whether that’s building new solutions or leveraging existing ones. Amazon Web Services, and Infrastructure Security in particular, operate at massive scale and as a result, demand the highest standards, passion, and discipline for information security and software engineering. A high level of ownership and accountability is a must for this role.

jobs.description:
The Rapid & Rural Logistics (R2L) team is seeking a Business Intelligence (BI) Engineer to join the Analytics Team. The R2L team, which includes Sub Same Day (SSD), covers a portfolio of high-growth businesses with unique fulfillment and operational capabilities. R2L exists solely to meet the goal of quick, accurate delivery, which is constantly being challenged and changed as we adapt to our customer demands and make improvements. To meet this goal, R2L must remain flexible at all times, adapting to all changes and growth that come our way. As a Business Intelligence (BI) Engineer, your focus will be on providing metrics, operational insights, and other analytical support to stakeholders for the Sub-Same Day (SSD) Delivery business. The Delivery BI team partners closely with Delivery and operations teams to influence the strategic direction of the business, drive operational excellence, and make sound investment and expansion decisions, with the primary goal of continuously improving the customer experience while driving long-term profitability for our portfolio of businesses. We experiment constantly, fail fast, and never stop learning.

The ideal candidate will draw upon advanced analytical and problem-solving skills, along with a passion for building solutions for automated operational management. The successful leader of this team will collaborate effectively with internal end-users, cross-functional stakeholders, and technical teams to enable data-driven decision-making. They must be a self-starter, show strong bias for action, be comfortable with ambiguity, and be able to think big and be creative to support a rapidly growing business. They will provide technical and business expertise in integrating data from various source systems and presenting it through an array of self-service applications. We look for candidates who are excellent communicators, inspiring leaders, self-motivated, flexible, hardworking, and who like to have fun. If you are excited about data, are results-oriented, and want to join a growing analytics team, this role is for you.

Key job responsibilities
The successful candidate is a seasoned data professional with a strong bias for action, data modeling, proficiency with data visualization tools, ETL, SQL, and scripting skills. In this role, you will help identify complex business problems, recognize opportunities, and implement solutions to improve operations and finance-facing systems, processes, and tools. The successful candidate will provide technical expertise in extracting, integrating, and analyzing critical data, clearly communicate any potential discrepancies and/or reporting downtime, including specific root causes and steps to resolution, and design and develop key performance metrics and indicators. The successful candidate will be a self-starter, quick learner, comfortable with ambiguity, results-oriented with strong attention to detail, insist on high standards, and adapt to a rapidly evolving environment. A typical day in this role provides an opportunity to build innovative data solutions with large-scale systems, identify high-priority work and projects that maximize results for key stakeholders, and solve complex problems that impact company-wide strategic decisions. Further, you will help automate processes by developing deep-dive tools, metrics, and dashboards to communicate these insights in real-time to business teams. Successful members of this team collaborate effectively with business customers and cross-functional data management teams to solve problems and implement new solutions.

A day in the life
A typical day in this role provides opportunities for building innovative solutions with large-scale data tools, identifying high-priority work and projects that maximize results for key stakeholders, and solving complex problems that impact company-wide strategic decisions.
• Take detailed requirements from stakeholders to deeply understand their current manual processes and pain points
• Think creatively to identify opportunities to solve problems using technology-driven solutions
• Design and implement optimal automation solutions to address stakeholder needs
• Respond to ad hoc requests and provide support as needed
• Thoroughly document all processes, solutions, and learnings to ensure knowledge capture and sharing

About the team
The Rapid and Rural Logistics team covers a portfolio of high-growth businesses with unique fulfillment and operational capabilities such as Sub Same Day (SSD). Our team needs to move fast and scale quickly with the growing businesses we support. We expect you to be able to identify one- vs two-way doors and make decisions on your own, requesting help when necessary. Strong bias for action is important. Technical skills must be balanced against people skills and business acumen so you can work with stakeholders to understand their needs and deliver the optimal BI solution.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of digital content management, communications, or content strategy experience
- Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
- Experience using content management systems and content tools
- Experience designing, planning, and delivering content solutions that meet the needs of technical business customers
- Experience building cross-functional partnerships and influencing stakeholders across the organization to act without having a direct reporting relationship
- Experience establishing governance frameworks or quality criteria for content at scale
jobs.description:
Join us on the Content Excellence team as a Senior Content Strategist and help define the standards and frameworks that govern AWS technical content.

At AWS, content consistency doesn't happen by accident; it requires clear specifications for how content is structured, organized, evaluated, and managed. As AI transforms content creation and management, these foundational rules become essential for authors and machines alike.

As part of the Technical Content Experience organization, the Content Excellence team is looking for a strategic thinker to define what good content looks like, and then partner with platform teams, content creators, and AI systems to ensure those standards are understood, adopted, and applied. The right candidate thinks in frameworks and systems and influences through clarity and collaboration. They excel at translating ambiguity into actionable specifications that other teams operationalize.

This role requires exceptional communication skills, strong analytical thinking, and the ability to influence without authority.

Key job responsibilities
-Define the content ecosystem strategy that outlines what customer needs AWS content must serve, how properties should be organized, and what content should be single-sourced.
-Create content architecture standards. Define the categories, taxonomy, and organizational structures that enable unified and intelligent content management.
-Manage the content model. Specify content types, components, and metadata that authors and AI systems use as their foundation.
-Establish content patterns. Document assembly rules for each content type so anyone can create or generate compliant first drafts.
-Maintain the Content Design System as the single source of truth for how AWS content should be created and managed.
-Define quality criteria that distinguishes good content from bad, enabling data-driven evaluation and routing decisions.
-Specify content lifecycle stages, triggers, and evaluation criteria that enable proactive management at scale.
-Define gold-standard criteria for AI training corpus curation.
-Drive alignment across stakeholders on definitions, criteria, and specifications.

About the team
The Content Excellence team ensures every piece of AWS technical content delivers a predictable, high-quality experience aligned to customer needs and business outcomes. We define the standards that make this possible at scale: content ecosystem strategy, content architecture, content models, content patterns, quality definitions, governance criteria, lifecycle frameworks, AI training corpus criteria, and the Content Design System.
jobs.description_short: Join us on the Content Excellence team as a Senior Content Strategist and help define the standards and frameworks that govern AWS technical content.At AWS, content consistency doesn't happen by accident;

jobs.description:
Have you ever ordered a product on Amazon and when that box with the smile arrived you wondered how it got to you so fast? Have you wondered where it came from and how much it cost Amazon to deliver it to you? If so, the WW Amazon Logistics, Business Analytics team is for you. We manage the delivery of tens of millions of products every week to Amazon’s customers, achieving on-time delivery in a cost-effective manner.

Amazon is looking for a passionate, results-oriented Data Engineer to help build the next generation of analytics for Amazon Logistics. In this role, you will work at the intersection of data engineering and AI to transform how Last Mile decisions are made. You will design and build scalable data systems and contribute to agentic solutions that enable leaders to access insights, metrics, and root cause analysis in a seamless and intuitive way.

As a Data Engineer on the Orbit (AMZL operational reporting and BI) team, you will operate in one of the most complex data and agentic solution environments at Amazon. You will focus on organizing and structuring large-scale datasets that power critical business metrics across Last Mile delivery, while enabling AI-driven use cases such as automated insights, RCA generation, and conversational analytics. You will work with modern AWS technologies and evolving AI capabilities (e.g., Bedrock, event-driven architectures, real-time data systems) to build reliable, low-latency, and cost-efficient data solutions. This is a high-impact role where you will partner closely with product, engineers, and business stakeholders to translate complex requirements into scalable data products.

You will thrive here if you:

1. Are excited about building data solutions that power AI and agentic workflows
2. Continuously learn and apply new technologies to solve real business problems
3. Can design simple, scalable solutions in complex and ambiguous environments
4. Care deeply about data quality, ownership, and building reliable systems at scale





Key job responsibilities
1. Build and deploy agentic AI solutions to improve Last Mile analytics and decision-making

2. Continuously evaluate and adopt evolving AI/ML technologies (e.g., Bedrock, Bedrock Agents / AgentCore, other AWS Services) and apply them to real business problems

3. Own and drive data quality initiatives for Lastmile, focusing on scalable and reliable solutions

4. Help the team scale by building simple, maintainable systems and mentoring junior engineers

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 10+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams experience
- 10+ years of program or project management experience
- 7+ years of managing, analyzing and communicating results to senior leadership experience
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience implementing repeatable processes and driving automation or standardization
- Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements
- Experience owning program strategy and end to end delivery
- Experience working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams
jobs.description:
Who are we?

Ready to build Amazon's next $100B business? We're not just entering shipping—we're transforming how billions of packages move around the globe. By evolving Amazon's legendary fulfillment engine into an agile, intelligent shipping network, we're creating a resilient system that continuously optimizes to deliver on time, every time, for every business and every customer. Every shipment is more than just a box—it spins the flywheel faster, continuously enhancing our logistics network through scale, speed, and intelligence. As we increase density and reach, we're revolutionizing shipping for ALL businesses while simultaneously strengthening Amazon's own delivery capabilities—reducing costs and accelerating deliveries across our entire ecosystem.

What will you do?

We're hiring a Principal Program Manager for our Integrations team in driving cross-organizational initiatives, influencing VP-level technology roadmaps, and unlocking millions in revenue through transformational improvements to our API ecosystem and developer experience.

You'll operate with complete autonomy to identify and remediate endemic problems in our processes, drive architectural decisions across Product, Commercial, Engineering, and Operations organizations, and establish the strategic mechanisms that enable Amazon Shipping to scale developer experience at the pace our business demands.

Working at the unique intersection where program leadership directly impacts revenue, you'll define multi-year technical and business roadmaps, influence resource allocation across VP organizations, and solve significantly complex problems where business and architectural strategies are undefined.

You'll collaborate closely with:
* Product Management teams defining API strategy and roadmaps
* Commercial teams driving enterprise customer onboarding
* Engineering organizations building integration platforms
* Operations teams supporting global shipping networks
* Principal Engineers and Architects on technical decisions
* VP-level leadership across multiple organizations

Who are you?

You're a seasoned program management leader with deep technical fluency in API ecosystems and integration patterns, combined with exceptional strategic judgment and proven ability to build consensus across diverse executive stakeholders. You have a track record of managing programs that span multiple VP organizations, operating independently in highly ambiguous environments where both the problem and solution are undefined.

Key attributes:
* Strategic thinker who connects technical improvements to business outcomes
* Technical depth in API development, system integration, and distributed architectures
* Executive presence with ability to influence VP-level audiences
* Data-driven decision maker who defines meaningful success metrics
* Strong owner who takes accountability for business impact beyond project delivery
* Exceptional communicator who translates complex technical concepts clearly
* Proven ability to identify and remediate endemic problems at scale

You thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments where technical decisions directly impact millions in revenue. You're passionate about solving endemic problems that affect global shipping operations while creating the integration playbook that scales Amazon Shipping worldwide.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Program Leadership & Roadmap Ownership

- Own the end-to-end strategy and execution for Amazon Shipping's API ecosystem and enterprise integration platform, working across Product, Commercial, Engineering, and Operations VP organizations to drive architectural decisions and organizational alignment
- Define multi-year technical and business roadmaps for developer experience improvements that directly impact shipper onboarding velocity, integration success rates, and API adoption growth
- Influence resource allocation and technology investments across multiple organizations to ensure alignment with integration strategy
- Identify and remediate endemic problems that create bottlenecks, limit scalability, or cause recurring developer friction, decomposing significantly complex challenges into straightforward solutions

Cross-Organizational Influence & Executive Engagement

- Build consensus across VP organizations through effective narratives and presentations, representing complex technical decisions and trade-offs clearly
- Manage executive-level escalations with excellent judgment, balancing technical constraints with business priorities
- Drive alignment between integration goals and organizational objectives across global stakeholders
- Partner with Principal Engineers and SDMs to ensure architectural decisions scale to match business growth projections

Technical Excellence & Architecture

- Drive large-scale programs that solve complex problems in authentication, rate limiting, error handling, monitoring, and developer tooling
- Evaluate system designs for scalability, performance, security, and operational excellence
- Lead architectural simplification initiatives that enable team independence and reduce technical debt
- Manage large-scale engineering efforts that remediate endemic technical problems with organization-wide impact

Metrics, Visibility & Business Impact

- Establish and own success metrics that connect technical improvements to business outcomes (integration time reduction, API adoption rates, developer satisfaction, revenue impact)
- Create mechanisms for continuous visibility and proactive risk identification across the integration platform
- Own quarterly business reviews that translate technical metrics into business impact for executive audiences
- Quantify business impact of integration improvements and communicate ROI to leadership

Team Development & Community Building

- Amplify impact by creating and optimizing critical mechanisms that enable teams to deliver independently
- Actively develop the program management community through mentoring, promotion assessments, and knowledge sharing
- Raise the bar for program management excellence across Amazon Shipping
- Streamline processes and drive adoption of best practices across multiple organizations

A day in the life
Your mornings start with strategic analysis—spotting patterns across the integration platform and quantifying business impact. You review API adoption metrics, integration success rates, and developer feedback to identify emerging trends and opportunities.

Mid-morning involves writing VP-level documents which drive technology roadmaps and priorities. You craft narratives that connect technical improvements to business outcomes, preparing for upcoming reviews and decision-making forums.

Afternoons focus on driving initiatives: partnering with Principal Solution Architects and SDMs on architecture decisions, influencing cross-org priorities, and preparing narratives for executives. You facilitate alignment meetings, resolve blockers, and ensure programs stay on track.

Late afternoons are spent managing escalations and engaging with enterprise partners. You work directly with key customers experiencing integration challenges, translating their feedback into actionable improvements.

Throughout the day, you shift between diving deep on technical problems and influencing stakeholders on business impacts. You balance strategic thinking with tactical execution, ensuring both immediate wins and long-term platform evolution.

You'll collaborate with engineering leaders building our adaptive technology, operations teams executing our delivery promise, commercial stakeholders focused on scaling our customer base, and product managers defining the future of our API ecosystem. This role demands someone who can toggle between strategic vision and tactical execution, turning integration challenges into opportunities for transformation.
jobs.description_short: Who are we?Ready to build Amazon's next $100B business? We're not just entering shipping—we're transforming how billions of packages move around the globe. By evolving Amazon's legendary fulfillment engine

jobs.description:
*This is for a new AR launch in Rockville, VA*

This position qualifies for a $40,000 internal launch bonus.

Eligibility Requirements: Candidates are ineligible to receive more than one Launch Bonus within a calendar year. Bonus payments are payable in accordance with the Company's standard payroll practice and subject to applicable withholding taxes.

The Sr. Operations Manager oversees all budgetary, people development, and operational objectives at the site. This role involves managing and leading a team of Operations and Area Managers, coaching and mentoring them to ensure performance objectives are met. The Sr. Operations Manager is also responsible for building and maintaining positive employee relations and developing leadership strength within the Fulfillment Center (FC). This position requires a strategic leader who can balance operational excellence with team development while driving results across all areas of the facility.

Responsibilities:
• Leads the development and implementation of quality improvement and process optimization initiatives, including Lean methodologies, to enhance operational efficiency within their functional area and enable the FC to consistently achieve and surpass business objectives.
• Responsible for overall performance of either Inbound or Outbound operation, including safety, quality and customer experience
• Sets and clarifies requirements and expectations for Operations and Area Managers. Measures performance, provides feedback, and holds Operations Managers accountable for their performance and the performance of their departments.
• Leverages Operations and Area Managers by sourcing and nurturing ideas, and rolling them into the creation of improvement plans for the functional area.
• Takes proactive steps to ensure that best practices are shared across all departments, shifts and among the network.
• Works closely with support staff (HR, Finance, Facilities, Safety, IT and other Sr. Operations Managers) to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives in their area, as well as providing needed support and resources for other staff initiatives.

Additional Job Elements:
• Lift and move totes up to 49 pounds each
• Regular bending, lifting, stretching and reaching both below the waist and above the head
• Walking around the FC with great frequency; FCs are over a quarter mile in length
• Continual standing and/or walking an average of 5 miles daily
• Ability to work in construction/distribution environments that may be noisy, unlit, unheated, not air-conditioned
• Able to access all areas of building (ascending and descending ladders, stairs, gangways safely and without limitation)
Responsibilities:
• Drives creation of quality initiatives, process change initiatives and other Lean initiatives to enable their functional area and ultimately the FC to meet and exceed business plan.
• Responsible for the overall safety, quality and performance and customer experience of either Inbound or Outbound operations.
• Will set and clarify requirements and expectations for Operations and Area Managers. Measures performance, provides feedback, and holds Ops Managers accountable for their performance and the performance of their departments.
• Leverages the Operations and Area Managers by sourcing and nurturing ideas, and rolling them into the creation of improvement plans for the functional area.
• Takes proactive steps to ensure that best practices are shared across all departments, shifts and among the network.
• Works closely with support staff (HR, Finance, Facilities, Safety, IT and other Sr. Operations Managers) to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives in their area, as well as providing needed support and resources for other staff initiatives.

jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team and become a critical guardian of technological infrastructure! As a Data Center Engineering Technician, you'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of mission-critical technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operations that power global digital experiences. Your expertise will directly contribute to maintaining an extraordinary 99.999% uptime, keeping the digital world running smoothly and efficiently.

Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.

We are seeking experienced Engineering Operations Technicians to join our data center operations team, specifically focusing on liquid cooling infrastructure and water distribution systems. This role is vital in maintaining the continuous operation of our advanced cooling systems that support critical data center operations.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.


Key job responsibilities
• Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, fire/life safety, and cooling equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
• Monitor and operate mission critical liquid cooling infrastructure
• Conduct glycol concentration testing and adjustments
• Operate and maintain water distribution systems, including water chemistry, pumps and piping networks
• Perform maintenance, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of liquid cooling systems
• Technical writing to support change management program
• Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
• On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
• Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts are usually 12-hours and may rotate on a predefined day, mid, or night schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations. Must be flexible on work schedule and willing to work holidays and weekends, as needed
• Role may support more than one location/site.

Physical Requirements:
• Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
• Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
• Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
• Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
• Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
• Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:
• Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
• Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
• Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.


A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

jobs.description:
Worldwide Grocery Stores (WWGS) - Grocery Distribution Center (GDC) team is looking for a Sr. Manager, Program Management to support scalable operational functions on our journey to become our customers most loved grocer. They will deliver results at both a strategic and tactical level specifically focused on our end to end execution and quality for Grocery Distribution Centers.

As the Sr. Manager, Program Management, you will be responsible for delivering and improving a GDC roadmap that significantly enhances service quality to Grocery Fulfillment Centers while reducing operational costs. You will achieve this by scaling a program that identifies and executes improvements, and by influencing partner teams to deliver them. This role requires a balance of analytical skills, communication, and development to support corporate and field stakeholders in meeting business needs. You will be a self-starter, skilled in driving actionable insights and clarity in a dynamic customer experience landscape.

The ideal candidate will think holistically about the optimal processes needed to support Amazon Grocery growth and provide recommendations on process improvements. You will drive business improvements by identifying short- and long-term solutions, streamlining actions for customers, and using data to identify business opportunities. In this role, you will help develop roadmaps and plans with partner teams to drive organization-wide goals.

The successful candidate will take a user-centric approach when solving process challenges, demonstrating both a strong business sense and a solid understanding of the technical landscape. You must thrive and succeed in an entrepreneurial environment. High judgment, the ability to influence, analytical talent, exceptional communication skills, and leadership are essential to success in this role. This position will support multiple regions and time zones.

Key job responsibilities
• Oversee the overall program execution, strategically driving teams within and outside your organization to deliver on goals.
• Help define the program's mission, vision, and guiding principles, then set objectives, analyze data, and drive quantifiable improvements.
• Work autonomously in an ambiguous environment, seeking to understand business problems, automation limitations, scaling factors, boundary conditions and reasons behind leadership decisions.
• Partner with teams across the business you support and beyond to source, allocate, and coordinate resources.
• Partner with customers, internal/external teams, and engineering teams to determine project priorities and next steps.
• Solve complex problems and proactively identify and mitigate risks before they become roadblocks.
• Communicate and impact critical business initiatives by working closely with program managers, business leaders, and the executive team.
• Develop, implement, and govern KPI reporting for a portfolio of programs, providing visibility into milestones and performance across all projects.
• Engage other Amazon business units globally to share best practices and improve end-to-end outcomes for Amazon customers.
• Demonstrate a "general manager/owner" mentality, rolling up your sleeves to ensure the success of the program.
• Create, communicate, and manage budget for projects.

jobs.description:
MULTIPLE POSITIONS AVAILABLE

Employer: AMAZON WEB SERVICES, INC.
Offered Position: Principal, Solutions Architect
Job Location: Seattle, Washington
Job Number: AMZN9082390

Position Responsibilities:

Architect solutions to significantly complex problems. Work in high ambiguity environments where business objectives and technology strategy are not defined. Influence customer's long-term business and technology strategies. Apply extensive experience and high judgment to deliver solutions with measurable, long-term impact. Apply extensive experience to build a resilience program to help customers architect and build resilient systems that align with their business goals.

Position Requirements:

Bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field and six years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation. Employer will accept eight years of experience in the job offered or a related occupation as equivalent to the Bachelor's degree and six years of experience. Must have three years of experience in the following skill(s): understanding of architectural and design principles for large-scale, complex solutions; experience decomposing complex problems into straightforward solutions; experience communicating technical concepts to technical and business audience ranging from a builder to the CxO; experience designing solutions that exemplify security, reliability, cost optimization, operational excellence, and performance efficiency; and experience building and operating a global program. 100% telecommuting permitted, work may be performed from anywhere in the U.S. Domestic and international travel up to 50% to perform role responsibilities may be required.

Amazon.com is an Equal Opportunity-Affirmative Action Employer – Minority / Female / Disability / Veteran / Gender Identity / Sexual Orientation.
